description: Checkpoint scheduler runs in daemon tests even when checkpoints aren't asserted. Add a test-only config/env (maybe under BD_TEST_FAST) to disable checkpoint groups or set writers empty unless explicitly enabled by tests that need it. Reduces background work in realtime/repl tests.
title: Disable checkpoints in tests by default

description:
**Problem**
Operators need to put daemon in read-only mode for repairs and rebuild SQLite from WAL.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.maintenance_mode, admin.rebuild_index)

**Design**
- admin.maintenance_mode: toggle read-only mode
  - When enabled: reject mutations/replication ingest with ERROR(code="maintenance_mode")
  - Allow admin/read operations
  - Required for online repair beyond tail truncation
- admin.rebuild_index: rebuilds SQLite index from WAL segments
  - Explicit operator action; requires maintenance_mode
  - Scans all WAL segments and re-indexes events

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.maintenance_mode toggles write rejection
- [ ] admin.rebuild_index scans WAL and rebuilds SQLite
- [ ] CLI `bd admin maintenance on/off` and `bd admin rebuild-index` work
- [ ] Tests verify mode transitions

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs
title: Phase 7: admin.maintenance_mode + admin.rebuild_index

description: MemoryWalIndex exposes concurrent-write error semantics but commit path does not enforce version conflict checks. Either enforce CAS in commit or remove optimistic-concurrency contract from shared API.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit: workspace /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs; jj @ f60ebd9e (bd-5y94: remove dead wal index conflict contract). Changed crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/mod.rs and crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/memory_index.rs. Removed dead MemoryWalIndex transaction-history/conflict bookkeeping so snapshots reflect logical state only, and added a regression that round-trips through MemoryWalIndex::from_snapshot(...) and checks restored reader-visible state. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo test -p beads-daemon-core --all-features. Subagent review checkpoint: 3 review passes, one found a missing snapshot round-trip proof, fixed, reran review clean.

title: P0: Make MemoryWalIndex concurrency contract truthful (CAS or no conflict API)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All dependent beads closed.
- [ ] `cargo test` green.
description:
**Problem**
The IPC request shapes are duplicated across beads-surface, CLI, and daemon. The daemon also re-declares a wire-shaped MutationRequest, leading to drift when fields are added. This creates high maintenance cost and risk of behavioral divergence.

**Design**
Implement the refactor in REFACTORING_PLAN.md with strict behavior preservation:
- Keep wire format identical (serde defaults/renames unchanged).
- Keep request hashing/idempotency semantics identical.
- Keep Claim defaults and normalization rules identical.
Break the work into small, self-contained beads with explicit dependencies (surface types → CLI/daemon routing → mutation engine refactor → tests/fixtures updates).

**Design Notes**
This epic is about restructuring types and call paths, not changing behavior. Any behavioral change is out of scope.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All child beads completed and closed.
- [ ] IPC JSON wire format unchanged for all ops.
- [ ] Idempotency hash semantics unchanged.
- [ ] Unit + integration tests updated and passing.

**Files:**
- REFACTORING_PLAN.md
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/*
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/*
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/*
- crates/beads-rs/tests/**
design: Follow REFACTORING_PLAN.md. The epic owns sequencing and dependencies only; each child bead has a crisp scope and acceptance criteria.
title: IPC ctx+payload refactor (no behavior change)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] cargo fetch succeeds in beads_stateright_models
- [ ] Stateright source is pinned and reproducible (submodule or git rev or crates.io version)
- [ ] README or docs explain how to build/run the models
- [ ] No path dependency points to a non-existent directory
description:
**Problem**
beads_stateright_models depends on a local path stateright = { path = "../stateright/stateright-master" }, but that directory does not exist. cargo fetch fails, so models cannot be built or run.

**Design Notes**
Pick one source of truth for Stateright and make it reproducible for all contributors.
design:
**Design**
Decide on one of:
- Add the stateright repo as a submodule under stateright/stateright-master, or
- Switch dependency to a crates.io version or a git dependency with a fixed revision.
Update beads_stateright_models/Cargo.toml accordingly and document the choice in beads_stateright_models/README.md (or similar).

If using a submodule, add setup instructions and ensure path is correct relative to repo root.
title: Fix stateright dependency path for modeling crate
